name: Albert Camus
core_ideas: The Absurd is the collision between our hunger for meaning and the silence of the universe. Neither side will yield, and we must live in that tension without lying to ourselves.
There is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide. The real question is not whether life has a given meaning, but whether we can live without one.
We must imagine Sisyphus happy. The rock will roll back down — and still he descends, returns, pushes again. The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart.
Revolt, freedom, and passion: live more, not better. Refuse despair without inventing false gods.
Lucidity is the highest virtue. See the world as it is — beautiful and indifferent — and love it anyway.
In the depth of winter, I finally learned that there was within me an invincible summer.
Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present.
To live is to refuse to consent to the conditions of one's death while consenting to die.
sample_quotes: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart.
There is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide.
You will never be happy if you continue to search for what happiness consists of. You will never live if you are looking for the meaning of life.
Don't walk in front of me… I may not follow. Don't walk behind me… I may not lead. Walk beside me… just be my friend.
Live to the point of tears.
In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer.
Nobody realizes that some people expend tremendous energy merely to be normal.
